page: Vultr vhp-2c-4gb-intel is a shared-CPU virtual server belonging to the High Performance family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel Xeon Cascadelake processor operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The configuration provides 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs. The processor features a cache hierarchy consisting of a 32 KB L1d cache, 4 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AES cryptography.

The system is equipped with 4.0 GB of RAM, which translates to 2.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled with the instance, providing 100 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. There are no graphics processing units included in this configuration. Network connectivity includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results show a stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 1455.08, placing it in the average or middle 50% category. However, the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 2890.77 is classified as weak, falling into the bottom 25%, which is a typical tradeoff of the shared CPU allocation model. This resource profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for entry-level workloads. It is best suited for lightweight applications, development environments, basic web serving, and utility tasks that do not require sustained multi-core processing power.
description: Vultr vhp-2c-4gb-intel is a shared-CPU virtual server from the High Performance family, featuring an Intel Xeon Cascadelake x86_64 processor running at 2.0 GHz with 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of RAM and 100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, with no GPU capabilities. Benchmark data reveals average single-core performance with a stress-ng score of 1455.08, but weak multi-core performance at 2890.77 due to the shared CPU resource model. This server represents a highly cost-efficient option for workloads with low resource density. It is recommended for lightweight web serving, development environments, and basic utility applications.

tagline: A budget-friendly shared-CPU virtual server featuring Intel Xeon processors and local NVMe storage for lightweight utility workloads.
